vix.note: VIX jumped about 13 percent to 19.49 on Tuesday from a prior close of 17.28, a contained move that reads as an orderly, chip-led de-risking rather than a panic; it remains far below the year high near 35.

macro: A Korea-led semiconductor and AI-memory rout deepens the mega-cap pullback. US chip stocks plunged Tuesday as a sharp selloff in South Korean technology spilled over and investors took profits in AI-memory names ahead of Micron's make-or-break earnings: SMH fell 7.0 percent, the leveraged SOXL collapsed 23 percent, and Nvidia, Intel and Broadcom dropped 3 to 6 percent. The damage stayed concentrated, though: Microsoft rose 1.8 percent, Berkshire, Disney and the Dow held firm, and the S&P 500 slipped just 1.4 percent as money rotated toward quality and defensives. VIX rose about 13 percent to roughly 19.5 (still orderly) while gold eased and silver tumbled 5.3 percent in a broad de-risk.
action: Stay patient and let the chip rout settle: lean on quality ballast (BRKB, MSFT, DIA, SPY), keep semis (SMH, NVDA, AVGO) on the watchlist for a steadier base near support rather than chasing the knife, and treat Micron's earnings as the next read on the AI-memory cycle.

long.h: Alphabet to Join the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Replacing Verizon on June 29
long.p: S&P Dow Jones Indices confirmed Alphabet will replace Verizon in the DJIA before the June 29 open, making it the fifth Magnificent Seven member in the price-weighted index. Inclusion adds a durable passive-flow bid and cements GOOGL as a core large-cap holding.

long.h: Nvidia Is Officially the Largest Stock in the World, Still Near a Market Multiple
long.p: Nvidia is now the biggest company on the planet yet trades at only a slight premium to the broad market, with management guiding to another large year of hyperscaler-driven growth in 2027. The debate is whether AI demand stays durable enough to support the valuation at this size.

long.h: Apple and Intel Sign Historic Chip Manufacturing Partnership
long.p: Intel will manufacture some of Apple's chips under a landmark foundry agreement that could add a major recurring revenue stream and validate Intel's turnaround. The stock is no longer cheap after a huge run, but the Apple relationship reframes the long-term thesis.

long.h: Broadcom: The Market's Tantrum Around AI Guidance Looks Misguided
long.p: Broadcom's AI revenue is locked in by multi-year custom-silicon and networking contracts, with FY27 AI sales seen topping $100 billion and order visibility into 2028. The note argues the selloff overlooks a near-monopoly in AI networking silicon.

long.h: Why This Trillion-Dollar Cloud Pioneer Looks Like a Better AI Buy Than Apple
long.p: Amazon is spending heavily to expand its computing footprint and trades far cheaper than Apple on one cash-flow measure, supporting a long-term AWS-and-AI thesis. The recent pullback ties to broad AI-capex anxiety rather than a change in fundamentals.

short.h: South Korea's Tech Rout Spills Into US Semiconductor ETFs Ahead of Micron's Make-or-Break Report
short.p: A sharp selloff in South Korean technology shares dragged US chip ETFs lower, with the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) down about 7 percent. Traders are de-risking memory and AI names just before Micron's closely watched earnings.

short.h: AMD and Intel Drop About 5 Percent, Nvidia Slips, in a Korea-Led Chip Selloff Bulls Call Healthy
short.p: Chip stocks led a sharp midday slide, with AMD and Intel off roughly 5 percent and Nvidia lower, as a Korean-led rout rippled through the group. Some strategists frame the drawdown as healthy profit-taking after a parabolic run rather than a top.

short.h: Why Tesla Stock Dropped on Tuesday
short.p: Tesla fell with high-beta tech as the chip rout hit risk appetite, with investors looking ahead to Q2 delivery numbers expected around July 2. Most analysts model deliveries between 400,000 and 420,000 units.

short.h: Netflix Is Down 32 Percent Since Reed Hastings Said He Was Leaving the Board
short.p: Netflix has slumped since its last report and the news that co-founder Reed Hastings is stepping off the board, and a Lionsgate buyout rumor has since collapsed. Bulls argue the stock now screens cheap near a 20 to 22x earnings multiple.

short.h: Nvidia Stalled Near $200: Is the Next Move a Breakout or a Breakdown?
short.p: Nvidia traded back to the closely watched $200 level, down about 4 percent on the day, as the chip selloff tested support. The level is shaping up as a near-term technical decision point for the AI leader.

notes.NVDA: Nvidia at $200.04, down 4.1 percent and back to a key technical level as a Korea-led chip rout hit the group; now officially the largest stock in the world yet still trading near a market multiple on a guided-strong 2027.
notes.MSFT: Microsoft at $373.94, up 1.8 percent and the standout mega-cap as money rotated into perceived AI-software quality and away from hardware during the chip selloff.
notes.INTC: Intel at $132.28, down 6.1 percent in the chip rout but giving back only part of a huge year-to-date run; the historic Apple foundry deal remains the structural story underpinning the re-rating.
notes.AVGO: Broadcom at $380.15, down 3.1 percent with the semis; bulls argue the AI-guidance tantrum is misguided given multi-year custom-silicon and networking contracts.
notes.QQQ: Nasdaq-100 ETF at $713.65, down 3.3 percent as semiconductors and AI-memory names led a broad tech pullback.
notes.SMH: Semiconductor ETF at $622.05, down 7.0 percent in a Korea-led, memory-driven rout ahead of Micron's earnings; the leveraged SOXL collapsed 23 percent on the day.
notes.SPY: S&P 500 ETF at $733.58, down just 1.4 percent as defensive breadth and a rotation into quality cushioned the chip-led selloff.
notes.AAPL: Apple at $294.30, down only 0.9 percent and relatively resilient; the new Intel foundry partnership and a near-record level keep the structural story intact.
notes.AMZN: Amazon at $234.11, up 0.6 percent and bucking the tech tape; heavy AI-capex spend and a cheap-versus-Apple valuation underpin the long-term cloud thesis.
notes.META: Meta at $562.20, down 0.3 percent and holding up well versus semis; the ad-and-AI engine remains the support even as capex scrutiny lingers.
notes.GOOGL: Alphabet at $346.13, down only 1.0 percent and a relative outperformer; confirmation it joins the Dow on June 29 (replacing Verizon) adds a structural passive-flow catalyst.
notes.TSLA: Tesla at $381.61, down 5.8 percent with high-beta names ahead of July 2 Q2 delivery numbers (consensus 400k to 420k); a volatile holding tied to robotaxi and Optimus optionality.
notes.NFLX: Netflix at $72.82, essentially flat but about 32 percent below its highs since the Hastings board exit and the collapsed Lionsgate rumor; bulls note a historically low 20 to 22x multiple.
notes.DIS: Disney at $103.54, up 1.1 percent and a defensive-rotation winner; parks and streaming profitability remain the swing factors.
notes.BRKB: Berkshire at $492.81, up 0.8 percent and acting as portfolio ballast, exactly the quality bid that outperforms on risk-off days.
notes.DIA: Dow ETF at $516.62, roughly flat and a relative haven; the index reshuffles on June 29 as Alphabet replaces Verizon.
notes.OIL: USO at $111.26, down 1.3 percent and well off the early-June highs above $135 as the geopolitical risk premium continues to bleed out.
notes.BTC: Bitcoin near $62,645, down about 2 percent and slipping toward the low-60Ks as risk assets de-risked alongside equities.
notes.GOLD: Gold near $4,149, down 0.8 percent and easing further from recent highs above $4,380 as real yields and a firmer dollar weigh.
notes.SILVER: Silver near $62.07, down 5.3 percent and the day's biggest commodity loser; its industrial and solar demand link makes it a high-beta casualty of the semiconductor and growth scare.
